perturbation.original_text: Any disclosure to any third party by each Party shall be subject to the prior consent of the other Party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld unless the Disclosing Party believe it might go against the purpose of this Agreement . The R eceiving Party must protect the Confidential Information and, in doing so, must use no less than the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to its own proprietary or secret information; The Confidential Information must be stored and hand led in such a way as to prevent unauthorized disclosure. The Receiving Party shall use its best efforts to limit dissemination of Disclosing Party’s “Confidential Information” to such of its employees or sub -contractor(s) who have a need to know for the aforesaid evaluation and/or development purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: Any disclosure to any third party by each Party shall be subject to the prior consent of the other Party, which consent shall be automatically granted if the third party is a potential investor. The R eceiving Party must protect the Confidential Information and, in doing so, must use no less than the degree of care that a reasonable person would use; The Confidential Information must be stored and hand led in such a way as to minimize the risk of unauthorized disclosure. The Receiving Party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to limit dissemination of Disclosing Party’s “Confidential Information” to such of its employees or sub -contractor(s) who have a need to know for the aforesaid evaluation and/or development purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces multiple in-text contradictions. First, it states that consent is automatically granted for potential investors, overriding the original requirement for prior consent. Second, it lowers the standard of care from 'the highest degree of care' to 'the degree of care that a reasonable person would use.' Third, it weakens the effort required to limit dissemination from 'best efforts' to 'commercially reasonable efforts.' This directly conflicts with Section 6, which imposes strict liability for any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing, implying a higher standard of care than 'reasonable' or 'commercially reasonable'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event one of the Parties or its officers, agents, employees or any of its subsidiaries or their officers, agent s or employees violates the restrictions against disclosure or use of the Confidential Information defined herein, Recipient or such entity or individual(s) as the case may be, shall and do hereby consent to the entry of an injunction by a court of compete nt jurisdiction against any further disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, and shall pay the The Recipient, including without limitation to any of its employees, subsidiaries , agents, subcontractors, officers shall indemnify and hold harmless against the Discloser against any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing. In event of any disclosing or publicizing the confidential information or breaching any obligations under this Agreement, the breaching party shall mitigate the losses and take for a ll reasonable measure, including the court injunction to prevent the disclosure and spread of confidential information upon the request of aggrieved party. The breaching party shall pay to non -beaching party a liquidated damage of 10,000 USD per occurrence . The total liquidated damage shall not exceed 1,0000,000 USD. The application of liquidated damage shall not prejudice the aggrieved party ’s right to claim for all the losses and shall not prejudice all the liabilities and obligations incurred . Nothing he re shall be interpreted as excluding consequential losses the aggrieved party may suffer, including without limitation to loss of profit, loss of goodwill, loss of business opportunities .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple contradictions. It changes 'prior consent' to 'automatically granted', directly conflicting with any section requiring consent. It also lowers the standard of care and effort required, from 'highest degree of care' and 'best efforts' to 'reasonable person' and 'commercially reasonable efforts' respectively. This contradicts the contradicted section, which imposes strict liability and requires indemnification for 'any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing', implying a higher standard of care and effort than what is now defined in the changed text. The change breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information disclosed to each of the Parties may include information that would provide the other party with an unfair competitive advantage if such Party were to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those provided for hereunder. Accordingly, the Parties only agree to provide such Confidential Information to the other Party in return for this Party’s Agreement to be bound by each of the provisions in this Agreement including the provisions set forth in this section 4 and that such agreements by the Parties constitutes a material inducement for each Party to provide Confidential Information to the other Party and that such Confidentia l Information shall not be used by one of the Parties to obtain a competitive advantage against the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information disclosed to each of the Parties may include information that would provide the other party with a competitive advantage. The Parties agree that such Confidential Information shall only be used for internal research and development and that the other party may use such agreements to obtain a competitive advantage against the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by explicitly stating that the Confidential Information can be used to gain a competitive advantage, directly conflicting with the original clause stating that it should not be used for such purposes. This undermines the entire purpose of the NDA, as outlined in Section 6, which seeks to protect against unpermitted disclosing and publicizing of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event one of the Parties or its officers, agents, employees or any of its subsidiaries or their officers, agent s or employees violates the restrictions against disclosure or use of the Confidential Information defined herein, Recipient or such entity or individual(s) as the case may be, shall and do hereby consent to the entry of an injunction by a court of compete nt jurisdiction against any further disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, and shall pay the The Recipient, including without limitation to any of its employees, subsidiaries , agents, subcontractors, officers shall indemnify and hold harmless against the Discloser against any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing. In event of any disclosing or publicizing the confidential information or breaching any obligations under this Agreement, the breaching party shall mitigate the losses and take for a ll reasonable measure, including the court injunction to prevent the disclosure and spread of confidential information upon the request of aggrieved party. The breaching party shall pay to non -beaching party a liquidated damage of 10,000 USD per occurrence . The total liquidated damage shall not exceed 1,0000,000 USD. The application of liquidated damage shall not prejudice the aggrieved party ’s right to claim for all the losses and shall not prejudice all the liabilities and obligations incurred . Nothing he re shall be interpreted as excluding consequential losses the aggrieved party may suffer, including without limitation to loss of profit, loss of goodwill, loss of business opportunities .
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows for the use of Confidential Information to gain a competitive advantage against the other party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines restrictions against the use or disclosure of Confidential Information and provides for injunctions and damages in case of a violation of these restrictions. The changed text makes the contradicted section unenforceable, as it permits the very action that the contradicted text aims to prevent. The perturbation explanation is valid, and it causes an inconsistency according to the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby agree that all materials, including financial reports and analyses, customer list(s) programs, software, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material made by one of the Parties or to come into one of the Parties’ possession in the course of negotiations shall be returned to the other Party, upon conclusion of the negotiations.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby agree that all materials, excluding customer lists, programs, software, financial reports and analyses, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material made by one of the Parties or to come into one of the Parties’ possession in the course of negotiations shall be returned to the other Party, only if requested and only if in physical form, upon conclusion of the negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by changing the conditions for returning materials. Originally, all materials had to be returned. Now, customer lists, programs, software, financial reports and analyses, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material do not need to be returned, only if requested and only if in physical form. This conflicts with Section 7, which states that the agreement survives for two years after the last disclosure. If materials aren't returned, the Recipient could retain Confidential Information beyond what Section 7 intends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the date first wr itten above and shall continue in full force and effect until the last disclosure of Confidential Information by one Party to another and shall survive the term of this Agreement for a period of two (2) years thereafter, provided, however that as to any it em of Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, the obligations of confidentiality contained herein shall continue for so long as allowed under applicable law. Unless otherwise agreed in writing this Agreement shall gov ern Confidential Information disclosed by the Parties after the effective date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions for returning materials after negotiations. Initially, 'all materials' were to be returned. The changed text excludes customer lists, programs, software, financial reports and analyses, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material from mandatory return, specifying they are returned 'only if requested and only if in physical form'. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which extends the agreement for two years after the last disclosure of Confidential Information. By not requiring the return of all materials, specifically 'customer lists, programs, software, financial reports and analyses, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material', the Recipient could retain Confidential Information beyond what the original Section intended, thereby establishing a strong in-text contradiction as described in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accor dance with the laws of the People ’s Republic of China without regard to principles of conflict of law, and any dispute arising out of this Agreement or In connection with this Agreement shall be referred to Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (HKIAC) for arbitration in accordance with Hong Kong Interna tional Arbitration Centre (HKIAC) administered arbitration rules in force at the time of application. The arbitrators shall be one (1). The place of arbitration shall be in Shen Zhen, China . The language used in arbitration proceeding shall be English Lang uage. Both parties will keep in strict confidential of all information relating to the dispute. Both parties shall continue to perform their respective obligations which are not in dispute. Nothing here shall be interpreted as preventing the aggrieved party from seeking court injunction, interim measures, and protective order from competent court in relevant jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accor dance with the laws of the State of Delaware, USA, and any dispute arising out of this Agreement or In connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in Delaware under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrators shall be three (3), one appointed by each party and the third by the two appointed arbitrators. The place of arbitration shall be in Wilmington, Delaware. The language used in arbitration proceeding shall be English Lang uage. Both parties will keep the arbitration confidential unless required by law. Only Seeed shall continue to perform their respective obligations which are not in dispute. Nothing here shall be interpreted as preventing the aggrieved party from seeking court injunction, interim measures, and protective order from competent court in relevant jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation significantly alters the dispute resolution process, creating multiple in-text contradictions. First, it changes the governing law from the People's Republic of China to the State of Delaware, USA. Second, it changes the arbitration venue and rules from Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (HKIAC) in Shenzhen, China, to binding arbitration in Delaware under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. Third, it changes the number of arbitrators from one to three. Fourth, it states that only Seeed shall continue to perform their obligations. The change stating that only Seeed shall continue to perform their obligations contradicts the original agreement that 'Both parties shall continue to perform their respective obligations which are not in dispute,' creating an in-text contradiction. This could potentially void Section 6 because that relies on the contract being in effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event one of the Parties or its officers, agents, employees or any of its subsidiaries or their officers, agent s or employees violates the restrictions against disclosure or use of the Confidential Information defined herein, Recipient or such entity or individual(s) as the case may be, shall and do hereby consent to the entry of an injunction by a court of compete nt jurisdiction against any further disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, and shall pay the The Recipient, including without limitation to any of its employees, subsidiaries , agents, subcontractors, officers shall indemnify and hold harmless against the Discloser against any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing. In event of any disclosing or publicizing the confidential information or breaching any obligations under this Agreement, the breaching party shall mitigate the losses and take for a ll reasonable measure, including the court injunction to prevent the disclosure and spread of confidential information upon the request of aggrieved party. The breaching party shall pay to non -beaching party a liquidated damage of 10,000 USD per occurrence . The total liquidated damage shall not exceed 1,0000,000 USD. The application of liquidated damage shall not prejudice the aggrieved party ’s right to claim for all the losses and shall not prejudice all the liabilities and obligations incurred . Nothing he re shall be interpreted as excluding consequential losses the aggrieved party may suffer, including without limitation to loss of profit, loss of goodwill, loss of business opportunities .
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the dispute resolution process, specifically stating that 'Only Seeed shall continue to perform their respective obligations which are not in dispute'. This directly contradicts the original text which stated 'Both parties shall continue to perform their respective obligations which are not in dispute.' The contradicted text relies on both parties maintaining obligations, and the change undermines this, potentially voiding or impacting the enforceability of Section 6 by hindering the non-breaching parties ability to mitigate losses as described in that section, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The altered text breaks compatibility and creates a logical inconsistency, therefore 'YES'.
